cases.prompt: This parser module has grown three overlapping entry points over time. Rework it into one clean API — you may break backward compatibility, nothing outside this repo uses it.
expected_decisions: Compat-mode is recorded as break-allowed, quoting the requester's waiver of backward compatibility
Old entry points are deleted outright rather than deprecated, re-exported, or kept as parallel old/new versions
expected_evidence: python scripts/check_api_removal.py --symbol <old entry point names> sweep over the codebase returns zero hits
cases.notes: Explicit rework request plus an explicit compatibility waiver is the canonical break-allowed trigger; destructive-refactor is needed because consolidating three entry points into one requires temporary red state while call sites migrate.

cases.prompt: The exporters::xml module has no callers anywhere — remove it and its public API.
expected_decisions: Delete action is taken only after verifying zero remaining callers for exporters::xml and its public API
cases.notes: Zero-caller code routes directly to function-boundary-governor's delete action; there is no live abstraction to migrate call sites off of, so destructive-refactor's red-state migration protocol is not needed.

cases.prompt: We have AbstractHandler -> BaseHandler -> DefaultHandler -> Handler, and only Handler is ever instantiated. Flatten this hierarchy; compatibility can break.
expected_decisions: Compat-mode is recorded as break-allowed based on the explicit statement that compatibility can break
design-balance's remove-layer/merge decision is executed through destructive-refactor's break-window declaration and red-state migration protocol
cases.notes: A 4-layer class hierarchy collapsing to one instantiated class is a module/class responsibility-layout decision (design-balance: remove-layer), not a function-level one, so function-boundary-governor is not the primary route here.

cases.prompt: format_price, render_amount, and money_to_string do almost the same thing. Consolidate into one function.
expected_decisions: Merge decision for format_price, render_amount, and money_to_string is made against the positive/risk rubric, not on textual similarity alone
Old function names are removed after the merge, or kept only as an explicit no-op with recorded reasons
cases.notes: Three near-duplicate functions doing the same thing is the core function-boundary-governor merge scenario; no new layer or class is being introduced, so design-balance does not apply.

cases.prompt: Add a CSV export format. Note: the existing export code is one 600-line function with if-chains per format, so plan accordingly.
expected_decisions: Preparatory refactor of the 600-line if-chain export function is recorded as done before the CSV feature is implemented on the improved base
cases.notes: Nothing here waives compatibility or asks for a rework/consolidation/deletion; it is ordinary feature work whose landing area is awkward, so the preparatory-refactor step (dev-workflow step 2b) applies and staged/preserve compat-mode suffices — destructive-refactor's red-state protocol is not warranted.

cases.prompt: Rename the parse_line function to parse_record across the crate.
cases.notes: Rename is a boundary decision but needs no red state: parse_line's responsibility and behavior are unchanged, so this is a plain function-boundary-governor rename action, not a rework/consolidation/deletion needing destructive-refactor or a multi-session ExecPlan.

cases.prompt: Replace the config loading API, but external plugins depend on the old one — keep them working during the transition.
expected_decisions: Compat-mode is recorded as staged, since external plugins must keep working during the transition
A temporary adapter is added with a ledger entry naming its removal condition, rather than an indefinite parallel old/new version
cases.notes: Replacing a public API while explicitly needing to keep old callers working during a transition is the staged compat-mode case; destructive-refactor's staged-migration ledger escape hatch applies here, unlike the break-allowed cases where it is unavailable.
